criteria_verification.criterion_text: Both wrappers in `src/forge/tools/graphiti.py`, each decorated with
criteria_verification.evidence: Created src/forge/tools/graphiti.py exporting guardkit_graphiti_add_context and guardkit_graphiti_query. Each is defined under @tool(parse_docstring=True). Verified at runtime: both are LangChain BaseTool instances, args_schema.model_fields includes doc_path/group/repo (and query/group/repo respectively), and the parsed description starts with 'Run `guardkit graphiti …`'. Tests TestDecoratorSurface.* pass (4/4).
criteria_verification.notes: Created src/forge/tools/graphiti.py exporting guardkit_graphiti_add_context and guardkit_graphiti_query. Each is defined under @tool(parse_docstring=True). Verified at runtime: both are LangChain BaseTool instances, args_schema.model_fields includes doc_path/group/repo (and query/group/repo respectively), and the parsed description starts with 'Run `guardkit graphiti …`'. Tests TestDecoratorSurface.* pass (4/4).

criteria_verification.evidence: _invoke_graphiti() passes literal subcommand='graphiti add-context' / 'graphiti query' to guardkit_run(). The captured kwargs in TestSubcommandPrefixBypassesResolver.* assert subcommand == 'graphiti add-context' / 'graphiti query' AND .startswith('graphiti '). This satisfies run()'s _is_graphiti_subcommand() head-token detector (TASK-GCI-008) which keys off subcommand.partition(' ')[0] == 'graphiti', so the resolver-skip branch fires.
criteria_verification.notes: _invoke_graphiti() passes literal subcommand='graphiti add-context' / 'graphiti query' to guardkit_run(). The captured kwargs in TestSubcommandPrefixBypassesResolver.* assert subcommand == 'graphiti add-context' / 'graphiti query' AND .startswith('graphiti '). This satisfies run()'s _is_graphiti_subcommand() head-token detector (TASK-GCI-008) which keys off subcommand.partition(' ')[0] == 'graphiti', so the resolver-skip branch fires.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: No `--context` flags appear in the assembled command line for either
criteria_verification.evidence: _invoke_graphiti() does NOT accept or thread an extra_context_paths kwarg through to guardkit_run() (deliberately omitted vs the GCI-009 helper). TestNoContextFlagInArgv.* assert '--context' not in kwargs['args'] AND kwargs.get('extra_context_paths') in (None, []) for both tools. Combined with AC-002 (resolver bypass at run() level), no --context token can appear in the assembled argv.
criteria_verification.notes: _invoke_graphiti() does NOT accept or thread an extra_context_paths kwarg through to guardkit_run() (deliberately omitted vs the GCI-009 helper). TestNoContextFlagInArgv.* assert '--context' not in kwargs['args'] AND kwargs.get('extra_context_paths') in (None, []) for both tools. Combined with AC-002 (resolver bypass at run() level), no --context token can appear in the assembled argv.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Both return a JSON string; on internal exception, return
criteria_verification.evidence: Successful path returns result.model_dump_json() (canonical GuardKitResult shape with status/duration_secs/stderr/exit_code). Exception path goes through _error_json() which uses json.dumps to build {'status':'error','error':'<ExcType>: <msg>','tool':'<name>'}. TestReturnIsJsonString.* (3 tests) verify success/failed/timeout return parseable JSON with the correct status. TestInternalExceptionsAreAbsorbed.* (3 tests) verify RuntimeError/ValueError raised inside guardkit_run get folded into status='error' with the type name and message, including a quote/backslash payload that would corrupt naive f-string concatenation.
criteria_verification.notes: Successful path returns result.model_dump_json() (canonical GuardKitResult shape with status/duration_secs/stderr/exit_code). Exception path goes through _error_json() which uses json.dumps to build {'status':'error','error':'<ExcType>: <msg>','tool':'<name>'}. TestReturnIsJsonString.* (3 tests) verify success/failed/timeout return parseable JSON with the correct status. TestInternalExceptionsAreAbsorbed.* (3 tests) verify RuntimeError/ValueError raised inside guardkit_run get folded into status='error' with the type name and message, including a quote/backslash payload that would corrupt naive f-string concatenation.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Function body wrapped in `try/except Exception as exc:`
criteria_verification.evidence: Each @tool body is wrapped in try/except Exception as exc: returning _error_json(exc, tool_name). The shared _invoke_graphiti() helper has its own try/except Exception as exc: as defence-in-depth (so callers get the JSON envelope even if the helper itself raises). TestInternalExceptionsAreAbsorbed.test_*_returns_error_json_when_run_raises confirms exceptions never propagate out.
criteria_verification.notes: Each @tool body is wrapped in try/except Exception as exc: returning _error_json(exc, tool_name). The shared _invoke_graphiti() helper has its own try/except Exception as exc: as defence-in-depth (so callers get the JSON envelope even if the helper itself raises). TestInternalExceptionsAreAbsorbed.test_*_returns_error_json_when_run_raises confirms exceptions never propagate out.

criteria_verification.evidence: Module-level logger built via _build_logger() prefers structlog.get_logger('forge.tools.graphiti') and falls back to a kwargs-accepting _StdlibStructLogger shim when structlog is absent (structlog is not declared in pyproject.toml). The success path calls logger.info('guardkit_graphiti_tool_invocation', tool_name=..., duration_ms=..., status=result.status); the exception path calls logger.exception(...) with status='error'. TestStructuredLogging.* (2 tests) patch logger.info to capture kwargs and assert the contractual triple is present.
criteria_verification.notes: Module-level logger built via _build_logger() prefers structlog.get_logger('forge.tools.graphiti') and falls back to a kwargs-accepting _StdlibStructLogger shim when structlog is absent (structlog is not declared in pyproject.toml). The success path calls logger.info('guardkit_graphiti_tool_invocation', tool_name=..., duration_ms=..., status=result.status); the exception path calls logger.exception(...) with status='error'. TestStructuredLogging.* (2 tests) patch logger.info to capture kwargs and assert the contractual triple is present.

Coverage: TestReturnIsJsonString (success/failed/timeout JSON shape), TestInternalExceptionsAreAbsorbed (exception → ADR-ARCH-025 envelope), TestNoContextFlagInArgv (no --context in args, no extra_context_paths), TestSubcommandPrefixBypassesResolver (subcommand starts with 'graphiti '), TestDecoratorSurface (parse_docstring schema), TestStructuredLogging (kwargs contract), TestModuleHygiene (no resolver import — uses AST walk to allow docstring mention but block actual imports), TestRepoConfinement (run() gets absolute repo_path under read_allowlist).
